title: Collision Events, Single Resolution, Fragments, Damage, and Lives · Python Build Room
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds ordered single-resolution collision response from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of ordered single-resolution collision response while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Separate collision facts from response decisions so detection stays pure and effects occur in an owned phase.
Assign pair identity and terminal ownership so score, deletion, sound, fragments, and telemetry happen once.
Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Detection Response
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 5565 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Detection Response.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate collision facts from response decisions so detection stays pure and effects occur in an owned phase. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Detection Response, keep Detection Response connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

Collision detection should immediately mutate every involved object. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Detection Response.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Detection Response for ordered single-resolution collision response.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Response Order
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 3946 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Specify response event ordering and test simultaneous projectile-rock, ship-rock, wave-clear, and life-loss cases.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-response-order-wx6wea" that uses case 5471 to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Response Order into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Response Order, keep Response Order connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-response-order-wx6wea
# Competency: asteroids-response-order.
# Case 5471: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_response_order_wx6wea(collisions: int = 3, resolved_once: int = 3, invulnerability_ms: int = 1200, duplicate_damage: int = 0, fragments_spawned: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_wx6wea = "wx6wea-aew6xw-5471"
    assert evidence_variant_wx6wea
    evidence_axes_wx6wea = (
        "playerwx6wea",
        "seed5471",
        "inputaew6xw",
        "framewx6wea5471",
        "state5471wx6wea",
        "playtestaew6xw5471",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_wx6wea)) == 6
    if collisions < 0 or resolved_once < 0:
        return False, collisions, "response-domain"
    if resolved_once != collisions:
        return False, resolved_once, "response-count"
    if invulnerability_ms < 0 or duplicate_damage > 0:
        return False, duplicate_damage, "damage-window"
    if fragments_spawned < collisions:
        return False, fragments_spawned, "fragment-policy"
    assert resolved_once == collisions
    return True, fragments_spawned, "single-response"
